query: SELECT itemId, name, organization, description, category, grantType, country, eligibility, website, phone, grantEmail, amount, status FROM grants LIMIT 10;
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user cK99zxEtGoTfC1g.7228e2c6a840 --database ne96tB4yURpkfMNLLJuy9T --execute SELECT itemId, name, organization, description, category, grantType, country, eligibility, website, phone, grantEmail, amount, status FROM grants LIMIT 10;

rows.name: 180 Medical Emotional Support and Resources
rows.description: Limitations People who use catheters or ostomy supplies (including new catheter users and individuals with colostomy, ileostomy, or urostomy) seeking emotional adjustment support, education, or product guidance.

rows.name: AAHD Frederick J. Krause Scholarship on Health and Disability
rows.organization: American Association on Health and Disability
rows.description: AAHD Scholarship Program The Frederick J. Krause Scholarship on Health and Disability recognizes students with disabilities who are working to advance the broad field of disability and health. The mission of the American Association on Health and Disability (AAHD) is to contribute to national, state, and local efforts to promote health and wellness in people with disabilities and identify effective intervention strategies that reduce health disparities between people with disabilities and the...

rows.name: Adaptive Equipment
rows.description: How it works: Donate Equipment May We Help welcomes donations of all types of clean and functional pediatric and adult equipment. There is no requirement to donate in order to receive equipment! If you have the equipment you would like to donate please bring it to 7501 Wooster Pike, Cincinnati, OH 45227 one of these donation times: Upcoming Donation Hours This is strictly a time to drop off equipment for donation. Unfortunately, our equipment team is unavailable to help find equipment during ...

rows.name: Adaptive Equipment Grant
rows.description: Limitations Competitive adaptive athletes of all ages; applicants list diagnosis (no medical proof required); financial situation considered to prioritize those facing socioeconomic barriers; recipients expected to use funding within the following calendar year (Fall 2025 cycle funds used by end of 2026).
rows.eligibility: Ages 0-100; General Diagnosis, Financial Hardship; Location: USA

rows.name: Additional funding and renewal grants
rows.description: You can apply for additional funding within the same initial 12-month grant eligibility period if you run out of funds and your balance is $0. Additional funding applies only to copay and health insurance premium grants. You can apply for a renewal grant within 30 days of the end of your initial 12-month eligibility period to start a new 12-month eligibility period if the fund is open. Renewal grants apply to all types of PAN grants: copay, health insurance premium, and transportation. Minimu...

rows.name: AFA Wounded Airmen & Guardians Program
rows.description: Limitations Must be an Airman or Guardian who meets one or more of the following criteria: - Listed as seriously or very seriously wounded, ill, or injured on official reports - Diagnosed with complex, service-related medical conditions, including PTSD, TBI, MST, cancer, chemical exposure, tick-borne illness, etc. - Purple Heart recipient - Undergoing or referred for a Medical Evaluation Board (MEB) - Reserve component members with extended Title 10 medical orders
rows.eligibility: Ages 0-100; General Diagnosis, Military/Veterans; Location: USA

rows.name: AFA Wounded Airmen & Guardians Travel Assistance Grants
rows.description: Limitations - Must be a medically retired Airman or Guardian enrolled in the AFW2 Program - Must be identified by AFW2 as someone who would benefit from attending a Wounded Warrior event
rows.eligibility: Ages 0-100; General Diagnosis, Military/Veterans; Location: USA

rows.name: Agility Program (formerly Agility Angels)
rows.description: Using the sport of dog agility, this program helps individuals with disabilities develop social skills, physical capabilities, and self-confidence. Agility Program participants direct dogs through obstacle courses within specific time limits. Minimum Age: 0 Maximum Age: 100 Financial Verification: Does NOT seem to be required Social Worker Verification: Does NOT seem to be required Letter of Medical Necessity: Does NOT seem to be required Situations Limited to the following situations: Diagno...
rows.eligibility: Ages 0-100; General Diagnosis; Location: Ohio, USA, Michigan
